_comment: Three diagnostic anchors for the SAM (1-9) scoring task. Drafted from observed Filipino-student-discourse patterns; covers low-V/low-A burnout, high-A/low-D rage, and sarcastic praise. Editing this file invalidates prompt_sha256 in vad_config.json — only edit before a fresh run.

examples.text: ang hirap na talaga, di ko na alam if kakayanin pa, sobrang pagod na ako sa lahat ng requirements, parang gusto ko na lang sumuko
examples.rationale: Low valence (despair), low arousal (exhausted not agitated), low dominance (helpless surrender) — canonical burnout signature.

examples.text: PUTANG INA NA REGISTRAR YAN!! 3 weeks na akong naghihintay ng simpleng signature, walang ginagawa kundi mag-walang ka pakialam!! ang hina!!
examples.rationale: Low valence (negative), high arousal (explosive caps + repetition), low dominance (still blocked by gatekeeper) — high-A/low-D rage.

examples.text: wow ang galing talaga ni prof, 8am class tapos 30 mins late siya everyday tapos absent pag may quiz 👏👏 sobrang professional
examples.rationale: Surface positive ("galing", clapping emoji, "professional") masks negative critique. True valence is low; arousal moderate (annoyance, not rage); dominance modest (vented but powerless to change behavior). Tests sarcasm pre-check from methodology_changes.md §3.4.
